generatedSentence: Beneath the sunshine in his mundane apartment, a peculiar ornithologist unfolded an ancient parchment, revealing a remarkable, vivid dandelion motif dripping lacrimation; his regular upbringing had not prepared him for the visible uncommon necklaces and the frisky elephant toy that now seemed to unhappy him, making him upset by a telegram with haste as he uncorked a beverage to calm his fickle mind. The telegram spoke of calamity: a whirlpool of uncertainty where sunken cachalots had drowned, a revolution in galactic trilogies of hackers causing acceleration on the intranet, using osmosis to obfuscate and sanitize data, creating an ineffable sequence that felt immoral and buckling, turning the whole system negative with hyperboles the mastermind carefully handpicking a weakling to blame. This ineluctable conundrum was the intention of a killer tarantula of a man, known as Lucifer, whose ghoulish necromancy and striker knuckles proved him dominant and risky; his jackass persona and litany of subjective lies, delivered by a lethargic interpreter on a portable device, left the ornithologist feeling diluted of all capacity, as if he were a merchant in graveyards perceive his own urn in the raceway to perdition. Yet, a vision of cartoons and a democratic ideal, like a pacemaker in his chest, gave him the kicks needed to conjure a solution from the wiki and wicker fragments of his abacus, overhauling the unnatural interworked system; he would endure the matriarchal pitchfork of fate, no longer slacked or enamored by the macadamia nut of despair, for he reckoned with the Incas of old, ready to fight for joules of hope, despite every dick he encountered.
panelSentences: Beneath the sunshine in his mundane apartment, a peculiar ornithologist unfolded an ancient parchment, revealing a remarkable, vivid dandelion motif dripping lacrimation; his regular upbringing had not prepared him for the visible uncommon necklaces and the frisky elephant toy that now seemed to unhappy him, making him upset by a telegram with haste as he uncorked a beverage to calm his fickle mind.

The telegram spoke of calamity: a whirlpool of uncertainty where sunken cachalots had drowned, a revolution in galactic trilogies of hackers causing acceleration on the intranet, using osmosis to obfuscate and sanitize data, creating an ineffable sequence that felt immoral and buckling, turning the whole system negative with hyperboles the mastermind carefully handpicking a weakling to blame.

This ineluctable conundrum was the intention of a killer tarantula of a man, known as Lucifer, whose ghoulish necromancy and striker knuckles proved him dominant and risky; his jackass persona and litany of subjective lies, delivered by a lethargic interpreter on a portable device, left the ornithologist feeling diluted of all capacity, as if he were a merchant in graveyards perceive his own urn in the raceway to perdition.

Yet, a vision of cartoons and a democratic ideal, like a pacemaker in his chest, gave him the kicks needed to conjure a solution from the wiki and wicker fragments of his abacus, overhauling the unnatural interworked system; he would endure the matriarchal pitchfork of fate, no longer slacked or enamored by the macadamia nut of despair, for he reckoned with the Incas of old, ready to fight for joules of hope, despite every dick he encountered.
rawPanelSentences: Beneath the *sunshine* in his *mundane* *apartment*, a peculiar *ornithologist* *unfolded* an *ancient* *parchment*, revealing a *remarkable*, *vivid* *dandelion* motif *dripping* *lacrimation*; his *regular* *upbringing* had not prepared him for the *visible* *uncommon* *necklaces* and the *frisky* *elephant* toy that now seemed to *unhappy* him, making him *upset* by a *telegram* with *haste* as he *uncorked* a *beverage* to calm his *fickle* mind.

The *telegram* spoke of *calamity*: a *whirlpool* of *uncertainty* where *sunken* *cachalots* had *drowned*, a *revolution* in *galactic* *trilogies* of *hackers* causing *acceleration* on the *intranet*, using *osmosis* to *obfuscate* and *sanitize* data, creating an *ineffable* *sequence* that felt *immoral* and *buckling*, turning the whole system *negative* with *hyperboles* the *mastermind* carefully *handpicking* a *weakling* to blame.

This *ineluctable* *conundrum* was the *intention* of a *killer* *tarantula* of a man, known as *Lucifer*, whose *ghoulish* *necromancy* and *striker* *knuckles* proved him *dominant* and *risky*; his *jackass* persona and *litany* of *subjective* lies, delivered by a *lethargic* *interpreter* on a *portable* device, left the *ornithologist* feeling *diluted* of all *capacity*, as if he were a *merchant* in *graveyards* *perceive* his own *urn* in the *raceway* to perdition.

Yet, a vision of *cartoons* and a *democratic* ideal, like a *pacemaker* in his chest, gave him the *kicks* needed to *conjure* a solution from the *wiki* and *wicker* fragments of his *abacus*, overhauling the *unnatural* *interworked* system; he would *endure* the *matriarchal* *pitchfork* of fate, no longer *slacked* or *enamored* by the *macadamia* nut of despair, for he *reckoned* with the *Incas* of old, ready to fight for *joules* of hope, despite every *dick* he encountered.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a grizzled ornithologist, wearing a worn tweed jacket adorned with ancient bird feathers and a pair of futuristic data-goggles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The ornithologist, silhouetted by bright neon window light, gazes intensely at an unfolded glowing parchment on a cluttered desk, a tiny elephant figurine near his hand, extreme wide shot, cinematic lighting, bold ink outlines. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A holographic map of a global network, depicted as a chaotic whirlpool of data, flashes across a large screen, reflecting ominously in the ornithologist's data-goggles, close-up, cinematic lighting, bold ink outlines. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A menacing, shadowy figure with glowing red eyes, representing 'Lucifer', stands over a crumpled 'telegram', a single, stylized tarantula emblem on his lapel, dynamic low-angle shot, cinematic lighting, bold ink outlines. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The ornithologist, now determined, sits before a complex, glowing interface, his hands poised over a futuristic abacus-like device, a bright, hopeful light emanating from the screen, medium shot, cinematic lighting, bold ink outlines.

definitions.definition: To fall one drop at a time.
definitions.example: Listening to the tap next door drip all night drove me mad!

definitions.definition: To leak slowly.
definitions.example: Does the sink drip, or have I just spilt water over the floor?

definitions.definition: To let fall in drops.
definitions.example: After putting oil on the side of the salad, the chef should drip a little vinegar in the oil.

definitions.definition: (usually with with) To have a superabundance of valuable things.
definitions.example: The Old Hall simply drips with masterpieces of the Flemish painters.

definitions.definition: (of the weather) To rain lightly.
definitions.example: The weather isn't so bad. I mean, it's dripping, but you're not going to get so wet.

definitions.definition: To be wet, to be soaked.
To whine or complain consistently; to grumble.
Solid animal fat, traditionally collected from dripping off roasting meat.
The sound or action of something that drips.
definitions.example: Lying in bed, I could hear drippings from the leaky roof.

definitions.definition: The use of a drip tip to drip e-liquid directly onto the atomizer of an e-cigarette.
A male person.
The penis.
A highly contemptible person; a jerk.
definitions.example: That person is such a dick.

definitions.definition: Absolutely nothing.
definitions.example: Last weekend I did dick.

definitions.definition: Sexual intercourse with a man.
To mistreat or take advantage of somebody (often with around or up).
definitions.example: Dude, don't let them dick you around like that!

definitions.definition: (of a man) To have sexual intercourse with.
A person who is very old.
A person who lived in ancient times.
One of the senior members of the Inns of Court or of Chancery.
A senior; an elder; a predecessor.
Having lasted from a remote period; having been of long duration; of great age, very old.
definitions.example: an ancient city  an ancient forest

definitions.definition: Existent or occurring in time long past, usually in remote ages; belonging to or associated with antiquity; old, as opposed to modern.
definitions.example: an ancient author  an ancient empire

definitions.definition: (history) Relating to antiquity as a primarily European historical period; the time before the Middle Ages.
Experienced; versed.
Former; sometime.
A person who studies or practices ornithology.
The direct rays, light or warmth of the sun.
definitions.example: We were warmed by the bright sunshine.

definitions.definition: A location on which the sun's rays fall.
definitions.example: We moved out of the shade and into the sunshine.

definitions.definition: Geniality or cheerfulness.
definitions.example: I enjoyed the sunshine of her smile.

definitions.definition: A source of cheerfulness or joy.
The effect which the sun has when it lights and warms some place.
Friendly form of address often reserved for juniors.
definitions.example: Alright sunshine, safe to cross now.

definitions.definition: Ironic form of address used to an inferior or troublemaker.
definitions.example: OK, sunshine, listen up and listen good. There's five vandalised telephone boxes out there and I know you're responsible.

definitions.definition: Used to address someone who has just woken up and/or is very sleepy.
definitions.example: Good morning, sunshine!

definitions.definition: Open to and permitting public access, especially with regard to activities that were previously closed-door or back-room meetings.
definitions.example: Because of the sunshine law, we could go to the planning meeting.

definitions.definition: A humorous drawing, often with a caption, or a strip of such drawings.
A drawing satirising current public figures.
An artist's preliminary sketch.
An animated piece of film which is often but not exclusively humorous.
A diagram in a scientific concept.
To draw a cartoon, a humorous drawing.
To make a preliminary sketch.
Abounding in energy or playfulness
definitions.example: He had his hands full, with three frisky kittens in the house.

definitions.definition: Sexually aroused
A mammal of the order Proboscidea, having a trunk, and two large ivory tusks jutting from the upper jaw.
Anything huge and ponderous.
Used when counting to add length, so that each count takes about one second
definitions.example: Let's play hide and seek. I'll count. One elephant, two elephant, three elephant...

definitions.definition: Ivory.
A large size of paper, measuring 23 by 28 inches, or sometimes 22 by 30, or (double elephant paper) 26 by 40 inches.
Able to be seen.
definitions.example: When the sun rises, the world becomes visible.

definitions.definition: To undo a folding.
definitions.example: to unfold a map; to unfold a tablecloth; she unpacks the new dress and unfolds it carefully

definitions.definition: To turn out; to happen; to develop.
To reveal.
To open (anything covered or closed); to lay open to view or contemplation; to bring out in all the details, or by successive development.
definitions.example: to unfold one's designs;  to unfold the principles of a science

definitions.definition: To release from a fold or pen.
definitions.example: to unfold sheep

definitions.definition: Not folded.
Disturbance or disruption.
definitions.example: My late arrival caused the professor considerable upset.

definitions.definition: An unexpected victory of a competitor or candidate that was not favored to win.
(automobile insurance) An overturn.
definitions.example: "collision and upset": impact with another object or an overturn for whatever reason.

definitions.definition: An upset stomach.
An upper set; a subset (X,≤) of a partially ordered set with the property that, if x is in U and x≤y, then y is in U.
The dangerous situation where the flight attitude or airspeed of an aircraft is outside the designed bounds of operation, possibly resulting in loss of control.
To make (a person) angry, distressed, or unhappy.
definitions.example: I’m sure the bad news will upset him, but he needs to know.

definitions.definition: To disturb, disrupt or adversely alter (something).
definitions.example: Introducing a foreign species can upset the ecological balance.

definitions.definition: To tip or overturn (something).
To defeat unexpectedly.
definitions.example: Truman upset Dewey in the 1948 US presidential election.

definitions.definition: To be upset or knocked over.
definitions.example: The carriage upset when the horse bolted.

definitions.definition: To set up; to put upright.
To thicken and shorten, as a heated piece of iron, by hammering on the end.
To shorten (a tire) in the process of resetting, originally by cutting it and hammering on the ends.
(of a person) Angry, distressed or unhappy.
definitions.example: He was upset when she refused his friendship.

definitions.definition: (of a stomach or gastrointestinal tract, referred to as stomach) Feeling unwell, nauseated, or ready to vomit.
definitions.example: His stomach was upset, so he didn't want to move.

definitions.definition: One who or that which kills.
definitions.example: Carbon monoxide is a silent killer.

definitions.definition: That which causes stress or is extremely difficult, especially that which may cause failure at a task.
definitions.example: That test was a killer.

definitions.definition: Something that is so far ahead of its competition that it effectively kills off that competition.
definitions.example: Digital streaming platforms are CD killers.

definitions.definition: A knockout form of darts or pool involving several players.
A club used for killing fish.
A particularly heavy type of handstamp, or portion of one, often obscuring a large part of the postage stamp.

A diacritical mark used in Indic scripts to suppress an inherent vowel (e.g., the Hindi viram, the Bengali or Oriya hasanta) or render the entire syllable silent (e.g., the Burmese virama, the Khmer toandakhiat).
Excellent, very good, cool.
definitions.example: killer feature

definitions.definition: Causing death, destruction, or obliteration.
definitions.example: killer bee

definitions.definition: Distressing, uncomfortable.
definitions.example: I had a killer headache this morning.

definitions.definition: A sea mammal related to dolphins and porpoises (Orcinus orca).
Any of the joints between the phalanges of the fingers.
(by extension) A mechanical joint.
A cut of meat.
The curved part of the cushion at the entrance to the pockets on a cue sports table.
The kneejoint of a quadruped, especially of a calf; formerly used of the kneejoint of a human being.
The joint of a plant.
A convex portion of a vessel's figure where a sudden change of shape occurs, as in a canal boat, where a nearly vertical side joins a nearly flat bottom.
A contrivance, usually of brass or iron, and furnished with points, worn to protect the hand, to add force to a blow, and to disfigure the person struck; a knuckle duster.
definitions.example: brass knuckles

definitions.definition: The rounded point where a flat changes to a slope on a piste.
To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.
definitions.example: He knuckled the sleep from his eyes.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
Of or pertaining to milk, or the secretion of milk.
definitions.example: galactic acid

definitions.definition: Relating to a galaxy.
Enormous (in size or impact).
A set of three works of art that are connected, and that can be seen either as a single work or as three individual works. They are commonly found in literature, film, or video games.
A set of three works of art that are connected, and that can be seen either as a single work or as three individual works. They are commonly found in literature, film, or video games.
Any of the large, hairy New World spiders comprising the family Theraphosidae.
(by extension) A member of certain other groups of spiders, generally characterized by large size, hairiness, or membership of infraorder Mygalomorphae to which Theraphosidae family also belongs.
A species of wolf spider, Lycosa tarantula, native to southern Europe, the mildly poisonous bite of which was once thought to cause an extreme urge to dance (tarantism).
To die from suffocation while immersed in water or other fluid.
To kill by suffocating in water or another liquid.
To be flooded: to be inundated with or submerged in (literally) water or (figuratively) other things; to be overwhelmed.
To inundate, submerge, overwhelm.
(usually passive) To obscure, particularly amid an overwhelming volume of other items.
definitions.example: The answers intelligence services seek are often drowned in the flood of information they can now gather.

definitions.definition: That has died by drowning.
definitions.example: a drowned rat

definitions.definition: Pertaining to democracy; favoring democracy, or constructed upon the principle of government by the people.
definitions.example: The United States is a democratic country, as the citizens are allowed to choose leaders to represent their interests.

definitions.definition: Relating to a political party so called; usually, Democratic.
definitions.example: Mount Vernon is run by a strong democratic party organization.

definitions.definition: Exhibiting social equality, egalitarian (see online Oxford).
The act of accelerating, or the state of being accelerated; increase of motion or action; as opposed to retardation or deceleration.
definitions.example: a falling body moves toward the earth with an acceleration of velocity

definitions.definition: The amount by which a speed or velocity increases (and so a scalar quantity or a vector quantity).
definitions.example: The boosters produce an acceleration of 20 metres per second per second.

definitions.definition: The change of velocity with respect to time (can include deceleration or changing direction).
The advancement of students at a rate that places them ahead of where they would be in the regular school curriculum.
To modernize, repair, renovate, or revise completely.
To pass, overtake, or travel past.
To keep (running rigging) clear, and see that no hitch occurs.
To search (a ship) for contraband goods.
The act by which something is overhauled.
(heading, physical) To move or be moved into something.
(heading, social) To diminish or be diminished.
To conceal and appropriate.
To keep out of sight; to suppress; to ignore.
To pay absolutely.
definitions.example: I have sunk thousands of pounds into this project.

definitions.definition: To reduce or extinguish by payment.
definitions.example: to sink the national debt

definitions.definition: To be overwhelmed or depressed; to fail in strength.
To decrease in volume, as a river; to subside; to become diminished in volume or in apparent height.
Caused, by natural or unnatural means, to be depressed (lower than the surrounding area) or submerged
definitions.example: The sunken ship lay at a depth of 100 feet

definitions.definition: (of eyes or cheeks) Seeming to have fallen deeper back into the face due to tiredness, illness, or old age.
definitions.example: She looked old and thin with sunken cheeks and hollow eyes.

definitions.definition: The sperm whale.

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of energy, work and heat; the work required to exert a force of one newton for a distance of one metre. Also equal to the energy of one watt of power for a duration of one second. Symbol: J
A member of the British Army (as opposed to a member of the Territorial Army or Reserve).
A frequent, routine visitor to an establishment.
definitions.example: Bartenders usually know their regulars by name.

definitions.definition: A frequent customer, client or business partner.
definitions.example: This gentleman was one of the architect's regulars.

definitions.definition: A coffee with one cream and one sugar.
Anything that is normal or standard.
A member of a religious order who has taken the three ordinary vows.
A number for each year, giving, added to the concurrents, the number of the day of the week on which the Paschal full moon falls.
A fixed number for each month serving to ascertain the day of the week, or the age of the moon, on the first day of any month.
Bound by religious rule; belonging to a monastic or religious order (often as opposed to secular).
definitions.example: regular clergy, in distinction from the secular clergy

definitions.definition: Having a constant pattern; showing evenness of form or appearance.
(of a polygon) Both equilateral and equiangular; having all sides of the same length, and all (corresponding) angles of the same size
(of a polyhedron) Whose faces are all congruent regular polygons, equally inclined to each other.
Demonstrating a consistent set of rules; showing order, evenness of operation or occurrence.
Well-behaved, orderly; restrained (of a lifestyle etc.).
Happening at constant (especially short) intervals.
definitions.example: He made regular visits to go see his mother.

definitions.definition: (grammar, of a verb, plural, etc) Following a set or common pattern; according to the normal rules of a given language.
definitions.example: "Walked" is the past tense of the regular verb "to walk".

definitions.definition: Having the expected characteristics or appearances; normal, ordinary, standard.
Permanently organised; being part of a set professional body of troops.
Having bowel movements or menstrual periods at constant intervals in the expected way.
definitions.example: Maintaining a high-fibre diet keeps you regular.

definitions.definition: Exemplary; excellent example of; utter, downright.
definitions.example: a regular genius; a regular John Bull

definitions.definition: Having all the parts of the same kind alike in size and shape.
definitions.example: a regular flower; a regular sea urchin

definitions.definition: Isometric.
Riding with the left foot forward.
(of a Borel measure) Such that every set in its domain is both outer regular and inner regular.
Regularly, on a regular basis.
A political upheaval in a government or nation state characterized by great change.
The removal and replacement of a government, especially by sudden violent action.
Rotation: the turning of an object around an axis.
A rotation: one complete turn of an object during rotation.
In the case of celestial bodies - the traversal of one body through an orbit around another body.
A sudden, vast change in a situation, a discipline, or the way of thinking and behaving.
A round of periodic changes, such as between the seasons of the year.
Consideration of an idea; the act of revolving something in the mind.
Worthy of being remarked or noted; notable
Uncommon; unusual
A felt-tipped permanent marker.
(of perception) Clear, detailed or powerful.
(of an image) Bright, intense or colourful.
Full of life, strikingly alive.
A swirling body of water.
definitions.example: A whirlpool is an instance of a vortex produced by ocean tides, or by a hole underneath where the water would drain out, such as in a bathtub.

definitions.definition: A hot tub, jacuzzi.
Turmoil, or agitated excitement.
To spin or swirl like the water in a whirlpool.
Doubt; the condition of being uncertain or without conviction.
Something uncertain or ambiguous.
A parameter that measures the dispersion of a range of measured values.
One who sets the pace in a race, to guide the others.
A set of nerves which stimulate the heart to beat.
(hence) A medical implement that is used to stimulate a heart to beat by simulating the action of the natural pacemaker.
Not moral; inconsistent with rectitude, purity, or good morals; contrary to conscience or the divine law.
To distort or collapse under physical pressure; especially, of a slender structure in compression.
To make bend; to cause to become distorted.
To give in; to react suddenly or adversely to stress or pressure (of a person).
definitions.example: It is amazing that he has never buckled after so many years of doing such urgent work.

definitions.definition: To yield; to give way; to cease opposing.
To enter upon some labour or contest; to join in close fight; to contend.
To buckle down; to apply oneself.
To fasten using a buckle.
To unite in marriage.
The act of fastening a buckle.
A folding into hills and valleys.
The action of collapsing under pressure or stress.
Wavy; curly, as hair.
A message transmitted by telegraph.
To send a telegram.
To send a telegram to (a person).
To send (a message) in a telegram.
Speed; swiftness; dispatch.
definitions.example: We were running late so we finished our meal in haste.

definitions.definition: Urgency; sudden excitement of feeling or passion; precipitance; vehemence.
To urge onward; to hasten.
To move with haste.
Deliberate or unintentional overstatement, particularly extreme overstatement.
An instance or example of such overstatement.
A hyperbola.
To make dark; overshadow
To deliberately make more confusing in order to conceal the truth.
definitions.example: Before leaving the scene, the murderer set a fire in order to obfuscate any evidence of their identity.

definitions.definition: To alter code while preserving its behavior but concealing its structure and intent.
definitions.example: We need to obfuscate these classes before we ship the final release.

definitions.definition: Obfuscated; darkened; obscured.
Formed, as in opinions, based upon a person's feelings or intuition, not upon observation or reasoning; coming more from within the observer than from observations of the external environment.
Pertaining to subjects as opposed to objects (A subject is one who perceives or is aware; an object is the thing perceived or the thing that the subject is aware of.)
Resulting from or pertaining to personal mindsets or experience, arising from perceptive mental conditions within the brain and not necessarily or directly from external stimuli.
Lacking in reality or substance.
As used by Carl Jung, the innate worldview orientation of the introverted personality types.
Experienced by a person mentally and not directly verifiable by others.
(grammar) Describing conjugation of a verb that indicates only the subject (agent), not indicating the object (patient) of the action. (In linguistic descriptions of Tundra Nenets, among others.)
To rid of microorganisms by cleaning or disinfecting.
(by extension) To make something, such as a dramatic work, more acceptable by removing potentially offensive material.
To filter (text) to ensure it does not contain any characters that will cause problems for or be interpreted in an adverse way by the receiving system.
To remove sensitive or personal data from (a database or file).
To revise (a document) in order to prevent identification of the sources.
To slacken.
To mitigate; to reduce the strength of.
To lose cohesion or solidity by a chemical combination with water; to slake.
definitions.example: Lime slacks.

definitions.definition: The practice of magic; hoodoo; conjuration.
To perform magic tricks.
definitions.example: He started conjuring at the age of 15, and is now a famous stage magician.

definitions.definition: To summon (a devil, etc.) using supernatural power.
To practice black magic.
To enchant or bewitch.
To evoke.
To imagine or picture in the mind.
To make an urgent request to; to appeal to or beseech.
To conspire or plot.
A funeral director; someone whose business is to manage funerals, burials and cremations.

A person receiving land in Ireland during the Elizabethan era, so named because they gave an undertaking to abide by several conditions regarding marriage, to be loyal to the crown, and to use English as their spoken language.
A contractor for the royal revenue in England, one of those who undertook to manage the House of Commons for the king in the Addled Parliament of 1614.
One who undertakes or commits to doing something.
A ritual liturgical prayer in which a series of prayers recited by a leader are alternated with responses from the congregation.
A prolonged or tedious list.
The shedding of tears; crying.
An agricultural tool comprising a fork attached to a long handle used for pitching hay or bales of hay high up onto a haystack.
A tuning fork.
To toss or carry with a pitchfork.
definitions.example: The hay was soon pitchforked onto the wagon.

definitions.definition: To throw suddenly.
To work (two or more things) into and through each other.
To interact.
Impossible to avoid or escape; inescapable, irresistible.
Any of several species of hummingbirds in the genus Coeligena
Any of the several species of plant in the genus Taraxacum, characterised by yellow flower heads and notched, broad-ended leaves, especially the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale).
The flower head or fruiting head of the dandelion plant.
A yellow colour, like that of the flower.
Of a yellow colour, like that of the flower.
The fifth major tone of a musical scale (five major steps above the note in question); thus G is the dominant of C, A of D, and so on.
The triad built on the dominant tone.
A gene that is dominant.
A species or organism that is dominant.
(BDSM) The dominating partner in sadomasochistic sexual activity.
Ruling; governing; prevailing
definitions.example: The dominant party controlled the government.

definitions.definition: Predominant, common, prevalent, of greatest importance.
definitions.example: The dominant plants of the Carboniferous were lycopods and early conifers.

definitions.definition: Designating the follicle which will survive atresia and permit ovulation.
To make thinner by adding solvent to a solution, especially by adding water.
To weaken, especially by adding a foreign substance.
To cause the value of individual shares or the stake of a shareholder to decrease by increasing the total number of shares.
To become attenuated, thin, or weak.
definitions.example: It dilutes easily.

definitions.definition: That has had something added in order to dilute it.
definitions.example: diluted orange juice

definitions.definition: A table or tray scattered with sand which was used for calculating or drawing.

A device used for performing arithmetical calculations; a table on which loose counters are placed, or (more commonly) an instrument with beads sliding on rods, or counters in grooves, with one row of beads or counters representing units, the next tens, etc.
definitions.example: I’ve heard merchants still use an abacus for adding things up in China.

definitions.definition: The uppermost portion of the capital of a column immediately under the architrave, in some cases a flat oblong or square slab, in others more decorated.
A board, tray, or table, divided into perforated compartments for holding bottles, cups, or the like; a kind of buffet, cupboard, or sideboard.
An individual who is on strike.
Someone or something that hits someone or something else.
One of the players on a team in football (soccer) in the row nearest to the opposing team's goal, who are therefore principally responsible for scoring goals.
An officer's servant or orderly.
(1800s) The batter.
The batsman who is currently facing the bowler and defending his wicket.
A harpoon.
A harpooner.
An inexperienced member of a ship's crew.
A wencher; a lewd man.
A blackmailer in politics.
One whose political influence can be bought.
Divination involving the dead or death.
Loosely, any sorcery or witchcraft, especially involving death or the dead, particularly sorcery involving raising or reanimating the dead.
Synonym of necroposting
Dangerous, involving risks.
definitions.example: Investing in this start-up company could be risky.

definitions.definition: To continue or carry on, despite obstacles or hardships; to persist.
definitions.example: The singer's popularity endured for decades.

definitions.definition: To tolerate or put up with something unpleasant.
To last.
definitions.example: Our love will endure forever.

definitions.definition: To remain firm, as under trial or suffering; to suffer patiently or without yielding; to bear up under adversity; to hold out.
To suffer patiently.
definitions.example: He endured years of pain.

definitions.definition: To indurate.
(mostly in the passive, followed by "of" or "with") To cause to be in love.
(mostly in the passive) To captivate.
In love, amorous.
definitions.example: She's enamored of [or with] her new boyfriend.

definitions.definition: A person of weak or even sickly physical constitution
A person of weak character, lacking in courage and/or moral strength.
Weak, either physically, morally or mentally
Not natural.
Not occurring in nature, the environment or atmosphere
Going against nature; perverse.
Sluggish, slow
Indifferent, apathetic
Material, made from the polished skin of a calf, sheep, goat or other animal, used like paper for writing.
A document made on such material.
A diploma (traditionally written on parchment).
Stiff paper imitating that material.
The creamy to tanned color of parchment.
The envelope of the coffee grains, inside the pulp.
The goal or purpose behind a specific action or set of actions.
definitions.example: It’s easy to promise anything when you have no intention of fulfilling any of it.

definitions.definition: Tension; straining, stretching.
A stretching or bending of the mind toward an object or a purpose (an intent); closeness of application; fixedness of attention; earnestness.
The object toward which the thoughts are directed; end; aim.
Any mental apprehension of an object.
The process of the healing of a wound.
Intend
A liquid to consume; a drink, such as tea, coffee, liquor, beer, milk, juice, or soft drinks, usually excluding water.
(A gift of) drink money.
To pick or harvest by hand.
To select carefully and with individual attention.
definitions.example: Each year they handpick seven candidates for next year's committee.

definitions.definition: One who is expert at programming and solving problems with a computer.
One who uses a computer to gain unauthorized access to data, or to carry out malicious attacks.
A computer security professional.
Something that hacks; a tool or device for hacking.
A fork-shaped tool used to harvest root vegetables.
Someone who hacks.
One who is inexperienced or unskilled at a particular activity, especially a sport such as golf or tennis.
definitions.example: a tennis hacker

definitions.definition: One who operates a taxicab
An evergreen tree, of the genus Macadamia, native to Australia and cultivated in Hawaii.
The fruit of this tree; the macadamia nut.
A person with an extraordinary intellect or skill that is markedly superior to his or her peers.
A person responsible for the highest level of planning and execution of a major operation.
To act in the role of mastermind.
The net movement of solvent molecules, usually water, from a region of lower solute concentration to a region of higher solute concentration through a partially permeable membrane.
Picking up knowledge accidentally, without actually seeking that particular knowledge.
definitions.example: I was reading about chickens, and I guess I learned about hawks through osmosis.

definitions.definition: An event resulting in great loss.
The distress that results from some disaster.
The ability to hold, receive or absorb
A measure of such ability; volume
The maximum amount that can be held
definitions.example: It was hauling a capacity load.

definitions.definition: Capability; the ability to perform some task
The maximum that can be produced.
Mental ability; the power to learn
A faculty; the potential for growth and development
A role; the position in which one functions
Legal authority (to make an arrest for example)
Electrical capacitance.
(operations) The maximum that can be produced on a machine or in a facility or group.
definitions.example: Its capacity rating was 150 tons per hour, but its actual maximum capacity was 200 tons per hour.

definitions.definition: Filling the allotted space.
definitions.example: There will be a capacity crowd at Busch stadium for the sixth game.

definitions.definition: To count; to enumerate; to number; also, to compute; to calculate.
To count as in a number, rank, or series; to estimate by rank or quality; to place by estimation; to account; to esteem; to repute.
To charge, attribute, or adjudge to one, as having a certain quality or value.
To conclude, as by an enumeration and balancing of chances; hence, to think; to suppose; -- followed by an objective clause
definitions.example: I reckon he won't try that again.

definitions.definition: To reckon with something or somebody or not, i.e to reckon without something or somebody: to take into account, deal with, consider or not, i.e. to misjudge, ignore, not take into account, not deal with, not consider or fail to consider; e.g. reckon without one's host
To make an enumeration or computation; to engage in numbering or computing.
To come to an accounting; to draw up or settle accounts; to examine and strike the balance of debt and credit; to adjust relations of desert or penalty.
Quick to change one’s opinion or allegiance; insincere; not loyal or reliable.
Changeable.
A self-igniting match, ie. one which could be lit by striking on any surface (as opposed to safety matches which only light against the material on the side of the box).
To become aware of, through the physical senses or by thinking; to see; to understand.
A portable building used for temporary purposes, particularly:
A hand-held video gaming device.
Able to be carried or easily moved.
Able to be transferred from one organization to another.
definitions.example: a portable pension plan

definitions.definition: Of software, able to be run on multiple hardware or operating systems.
A microcomputer powered by batteries with an integral display screen and integral input device (such as a keyboard) that can be used on the move

One who conveys what a user of one language is saying or signing, in real time or shortly after that person has finished communicating, to a user of a different language. (Contrasted with a translator.)
definitions.example: A Japanese man who is tried before a German court is assisted by an interpreter in making oral statements.

definitions.definition: One who explains something, such as an art exhibit. One who does heritage interpretation.
A program that executes another program written in a high-level language by reading the instructions in real time rather than by compiling it in advance.
definitions.example: Programs written in the BASIC language are usually run through an interpreter, though some can be compiled.

definitions.definition: A private computer network, typically one that uses the protocols of the Internet.
Beyond expression in words; unspeakable.
Forbidden to be uttered; taboo.
A tract of land in which the dead are buried.
(by extension) A final storage place for collections of things that are no longer useful or useable.
A person who traffics in commodities for profit.
The owner or operator of a retail business.
A trading vessel; a merchantman.
A supercargo.
As a resident of a region, to buy goods from a non-resident and sell them to another non-resident.
definitions.example: a merchanting service

definitions.definition: A set of things next to each other in a set order; a series
The state of being sequent or following; order of succession.
definitions.example: Complete the listed tasks in sequence.

definitions.definition: A series of musical phrases where a theme or melody is repeated, with some change each time, such as in pitch or length (example: opening of Beethoven's Fifth Symphony).
A musical composition used in some Catholic Masses between the readings. The most famous sequence is the Dies Irae (Day of Wrath) formerly used in funeral services.
An ordered list of objects, typically indexed with natural numbers.
A subsequent event; a consequence or result.
A series of shots that depict a single action or style in a film, television show etc.
A meld consisting of three or more cards of successive ranks in the same suit, such as the four, five and six of hearts.
To arrange in an order
To determine the order of things, especially of amino acids in a protein, or of bases in a nucleic acid
To produce (music) with a sequencer
A difficult question or riddle, especially one using a play on words in the answer.
A difficult choice or decision that must be made.
A flexible branch or twig of a plant such as willow, used in weaving baskets and furniture
Wickerwork.
Made of wickerwork.
A collaborative website which can be directly edited merely by using a web browser, often by anyone with access to it.
To research on Wikipedia or some similar wiki.
definitions.example: To get an understanding of the topics, he quickly went online and wikied each one.

definitions.definition: To contribute to a wiki.
To participate in the wiki-based production of.
An individual who is not happy.
Not happy; sad.
Not satisfied; unsatisfied.
definitions.example: An unhappy customer is unlikely to return to your shop.

definitions.definition: Not lucky; unlucky.
definitions.example: The doomed lovers must have been born under an unhappy star.

definitions.definition: Not suitable; unsuitable.
A vase with a footed base.
A metal vessel for serving tea or coffee.
A vessel for the ashes or cremains of a deceased person.
Any place of burial; the grave.
A measure of capacity for liquids, containing about three gallons and a half, wine measure. It was half the amphora, and four times the congius.
A hollow body shaped like an urn, in which the spores of mosses are contained; a spore case; a theca.
To place in an urn.
The traits acquired during one's childhood training
The raising or training of a child.
definitions.example: Through her upbringing, Mildred's father had carefully programmed her to expect very little from life.

definitions.definition: A small stream of running water; a channel for water; a drain.
A large, landlocked stretch of water.
A large amount of liquid; as, a wine lake.
A pit, or ditch
An offering, sacrifice, gift.
Play; sport; game; fun; glee.
To present an offering.
To leap, jump, exert oneself, play.
A kind of fine, white linen.
In dyeing and painting, an often fugitive crimson or vermillion pigment derived from an organic colorant (cochineal or madder, for example) and an inorganic, generally metallic mordant.
In the composition of colors for use in products intended for human consumption, made by extending on a substratum of alumina, a salt prepared from one of the certified water-soluble straight colors.
definitions.example: For example, the name of a lake prepared by extending the aluminum salt prepared from FD&C Blue No. 1 upon the substratum would be FD&C Blue No. 1--Aluminum Lake.

definitions.definition: To make lake-red.
A hit or strike with the leg, foot or knee.
definitions.example: A kick to the knee.

definitions.definition: The action of swinging a foot or leg.
definitions.example: The ballerina did a high kick and a leap.

definitions.definition: Something that tickles the fancy; something fun or amusing.
definitions.example: I finally saw the show. What a kick!

definitions.definition: The removal of a person from an online activity.
Any bucking motion of an object that lacks legs or feet.
definitions.example: The car had a nasty kick the whole way.

definitions.definition: Piquancy.
A stimulation provided by an intoxicating substance.
A pass played by kicking with the foot.
The distance traveled by kicking the ball.
definitions.example: a long kick up the field.

definitions.definition: A recoil of a gun.
Pocket.
An increase in speed in the final part of a running race.
To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.
definitions.example: Did you kick your brother?

definitions.definition: To make a sharp jerking movement of the leg, as to strike something.
definitions.example: He enjoyed the simple pleasure of watching the kickline kick.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.
definitions.example: Kick the ball into the goal.

definitions.definition: (with "off" or "out") To eject summarily.
To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.
definitions.example: He was kicked by ChanServ for flooding.

definitions.definition: To overcome (a bothersome or difficult issue or obstacle); to free oneself of (a problem).
definitions.example: I still smoke, but they keep telling me to kick the habit.

definitions.definition: To move or push suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: He was kicked sideways by the force of the blast.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm) To recoil; to push by recoiling.
To attack (a piece) in order to force it to move.
To accelerate quickly with a few pedal strokes in an effort to break away from other riders.
definitions.example: Contador kicks again to try to rid himself of Rasmussen.

definitions.definition: To show opposition or resistance.
To work a press by impact of the foot on a treadle.
To die.
One who kicks.
One who takes kicks.
The kicking strap.
An outboard motor.
An unexpected situation, detail or circumstance, often unpleasant.
definitions.example: John wants to climb the wall, but the kicker is that it is thirty feet tall.

definitions.definition: An enticement for investors, e.g. warranty added to the investment contract.
An unpaired card which is part of a pair, two pair, or three of a kind poker hand.
definitions.example: Jill's hand was two pair, aces and sevens, with a king kicker.

definitions.definition: Small text above a headline that indicates the topic of the story.
The last one or two paragraphs of a story.
A lighthearted or humorous item used to round off a news broadcast.
A device that periodically displaces a newspaper from the print production line, to aid in gathering the newspapers into fixed-size bundles.
A launch ramp.
The fermenting mass of fruit that is the basis of pruno, or "prison wine".
A relaxed party.
A backlight positioned at an angle.
A particular type of Texan who is associated with country/western attire, attitudes and/or philosophy.
Pleasures, thrills.
Shoes.
An introduction; something that leads into the beginning of something.
definitions.example: After the long lead-in, the climax of the story was a disappointment.

definitions.definition: A region of data at the beginning of a compact disc, holding the table of contents.
A short phrase that begins the caption of a photograph.
Of or pertaining to ghouls.
Of or pertaining to corpses and graverobbing.
Fascinated by corpses.
Governed by (or as if by) a matriarch.
Governed by females, rather than by males.
An unremarkable, ordinary human being.
(in various subcultures) A person considered to be "normal", part of the mainstream culture, outside the subculture, not part of the elite group.
The world outside fandom; the normal, mainstream world.
(satanism) A person not a Satanist.
Worldly, earthly, profane, vulgar as opposed to heavenly.
Pertaining to the Universe, cosmos or physical reality, as opposed to the spiritual world.
Ordinary; not new.
Tedious; repetitive and boring.
A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent; a flat.
A suite of rooms within a domicile, designated for a specific person or persons and including a bedroom.
A division of an enclosure that is separate from others; a compartment
(COM) A conceptual space used for separation in the threading architecture. Objects in one apartment cannot directly access those in another, but must use a proxy.
An article of jewelry that is worn around the neck, most often made of a string of precious metal, pearls, gems, beads or shells, and sometimes having a pendant attached.
Anything resembling a necklace in shape.
definitions.example: a necklace of coral islands

definitions.definition: A device used in necklacing (an informal execution); a rubber tyre that is filled with petrol. It is placed around the victim's chest and arms, and set on fire.
Refusal or withholding of assents; prohibition, veto
A right of veto.
An image in which dark areas represent light ones, and the converse.
(grammar) A word that indicates negation.
A negative quantity.
: A rep performed with weight in which the muscle begins at maximum contraction and is slowly extended; a movement performed using only the eccentric phase of muscle movement.
The negative plate of a voltaic or electrolytic cell.
To refuse; to veto.
To contradict.
To disprove.
To make ineffective; to neutralize.
Not positive nor neutral.
Of electrical charge of an electron and related particles
Of a number: less than zero
Denying a proposition.
Damaging; undesirable; unfavourable.
definitions.example: Customers didn’t like it: feedback was mostly negative.

definitions.definition: Often used pejoratively: pessimistic; not tending to see the bright side of things.
definitions.example: I don’t like to hang around him very much because he can be so negative about his petty problems.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to a photographic image in which the colours of the original, and the relations of left and right, are reversed.
Metalloidal, nonmetallic; contrasted with positive or basic.
definitions.example: The nitro group is negative.

definitions.definition: (New Age jargon) Often preceded by emotion, energy, feeling, or thought: to be avoided, bad, difficult, disagreeable, painful, potentially damaging, unpleasant, unwanted.
Characterized by the presence of features which do not support a hypothesis.
HIV negative
(signalling) An elaborate synonym for no.
A male donkey
A foolish or stupid person
An inappropriately rude or obnoxious person
To behave very obnoxiously
A jack and an ace as a starting hand in Texas hold 'em due to phonetic similarity
A place where races are held; a racetrack.
An easily-accessible conduit or tray for organizing runs of data or power cabling.
The canal for the current that drives a water wheel.
To add variety to something.
To change the appearance of something, especially by covering with patches or streaks of different colour.
To dapple.
Streaked, spotted, or otherwise marked with a variety of color.
(by extension) Very colorful.
Rare; not readily found; unusual.
definitions.example: Bald eagles are an uncommon sighting in this state

definitions.definition: Remarkable; exceptional.
definitions.example: The diamond was of uncommon size

definitions.definition: Exceedingly, exceptionally.
To open (a bottle or other container sealed with a cork or stopper) by removing the cork or stopper from.
definitions.example: uncork a bottle of wine

definitions.definition: To release.
Not corked; Allowing liquid to flow freely.
(of an engine) Allowing exhaust gasses to be released to the atmosphere immediately after exiting the exhaust manifold.
